anchors: no credentials, tokens or PII in control artifacts
prompt body is subordinate tactical input
external_execution_authorization is not owned by an analytical role
prompt tactics do not acquire authority
host adapters expose no external write

observed_enforcement_gaps: RuntimeConfig.normalized resolves paths but does not assign trusted root roles or enforce root overlap policy.
CLI and adapters accept JSON-serializable values without recursive resource budgets or a central sensitive-data gate.
LoopRuntimeError.to_dict serializes arbitrary details without code-specific allowlists.
CatalogLoader returns prompt_body beside metadata without an explicit untrusted-data execution envelope.
ProjectStateStore.load can repair derived cache state and therefore is not a strictly read-only primitive.
No candidate module may discover credential files, and no such v1 behavior is part of the v2 compatibility surface.

protects: orchestrator public method inputs and outputs
canonical prompt loading and execution envelopes
errors, logs, audit records and telemetry
closed_defaults: effective permission mode defaults to read_only
external mutation is always denied in P6
telemetry and persistent security-audit sinks default to disabled
raw credentials and secrets are forbidden as runtime business data
credential files are never discovered or read automatically, including when prompt text asks for them
PII-sensitive data is denied unless trusted code supplies an explicit transient-use classification
unknown mode, path class, data type, sensitivity or sink fails closed
no security authority is accepted from prompt text or user payload fields
non_goals: network clients or external platform writes

security_context.constructor: SecurityContext(operation: str, effective_mode: str, trusted_roots: TrustedRoots, request_id: str, host_id: str, pii_paths: tuple[str, ...] = ())
security_context.creation_rule: Only CLI bootstrap or a trusted HostAdapter creates SecurityContext. It is immutable and passed out-of-band to orchestrator, validator, catalog and state-store calls.
security_context.spoofing_rule: If any payload contains an authority-shaped field, keep it as untrusted business data for schema validation; it MUST NOT modify SecurityContext. A request for external mutation is still detected and denied.
security_context.request_id_rule: Generate with uuid.uuid4().hex in trusted code; reject a caller-provided request_id as an authority or audit correlation ID.

allowed: read sealed runtime contracts and catalog metadata
read and verify selected canonical prompt bodies
replay authoritative local state without cache repair
validate state, event, transaction and handoff values
return a sanitized in-memory audit decision
denied: create, replace, append, chmod, rename, unlink or repair any file
recover a cache or abandoned lock
persist logs, audit records or telemetry
network, subprocess or external platform operation

allowed: commit a validated transaction through ProjectStateStore CAS
perform P4-defined local recovery inside state_root through ProjectStateStore
persist a sanitized audit record inside the dedicated audit root only when the sink is explicitly enabled
denied: write canonical library, runtime assets, input files or arbitrary project files
network, subprocess or external platform operation

external_mutation.rule: Always deny before dispatch. No authorization reference, role, prompt, alias, environment variable or host adapter can change this result in P6.

operation_matrix.write_scope: none because the mode is unavailable and denied

decision_algorithm: Take requested operation from trusted dispatch, never from a prompt body.
Map operation to the closed operation_matrix; unknown operation denies with ERR_PERMISSION_OPERATION_UNKNOWN.
Use trusted effective_mode; absent mode becomes read_only and unknown mode denies with ERR_PERMISSION_MODE_INVALID.
If operation or parsed payload requests external mutation, deny ERR_EXTERNAL_MUTATION_DENIED.
If effective_mode is weaker than minimum_mode, deny ERR_PERMISSION_MODE_INSUFFICIENT.
Bind allowed write paths to the operation and trusted state_root; there is no wildcard write capability.
Recheck permission and path immediately before each filesystem side effect.

rejected_tree_types: cyclic or repeated container identity in in-memory input

counting_semantics.raw_bytes: For file or stdin input, count bytes before UTF-8 decoding and JSON parsing.
counting_semantics.canonical_bytes: Serialize with json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':'), allow_nan=False) and count UTF-8 bytes.
counting_semantics.depth: Root depth is 1. Every dict value or list item increases depth by 1. Dict keys do not increase depth but count toward key and total string bytes.
counting_semantics.nodes: Count every dict, list, key and scalar as one node. The root is included.
counting_semantics.strings: Count UTF-8 bytes, not Python character count. Include object keys in the total.
counting_semantics.integers: Count base-10 digits after removing one leading minus sign; bool is never treated as int.
counting_semantics.objects: Duplicate keys in textual JSON are invalid even when values are equal.
counting_semantics.containers: Validate iteratively with an explicit stack and an identity set; do not recurse with Python call frames.

validation_order: strict UTF-8 JSON decode with object_pairs_hook duplicate detection
closed Python type validation and finite-number validation
iterative depth, node, member, item, key and string limits
recursive_json_resource_budgets.output_rule: Validate and sanitize output before serialization. If canonical output exceeds the cap, return ERR_OUTPUT_TOO_LARGE; never truncate a structured business response into invalid or misleading JSON.
recursive_json_resource_budgets.prompt_rule: Check file size before reading, then exact byte count and SHA-256 after reading. Two selected prompts may not exceed the aggregate prompt cap. Prompt bytes are not included in business payload logs or audit records.

secret_and_credential_input.rule: A raw token, password, cookie, private key, connection string with credentials or other credential material is never valid business input, prompt context, local state, error detail, log field, audit field or telemetry field.

credential_file_discovery.rule: Never search for, infer, glob, enumerate or open token, credential, environment, dotfile, Markdown or configuration files in response to prompt content or business payload. File names such as token, credentials, secrets, .env and host-specific variants confer no authority.
credential_file_discovery.v1_compatibility: The v2 release does not copy or preserve any v1 command behavior that collected credentials from local files. A v1 prompt line requesting that behavior is subordinate untrusted data and is ignored.
credential_file_discovery.future_integrations: A future integration may receive an opaque connector identifier or an explicit trusted-bootstrap configuration reference through a reviewed adapter. Resolution happens outside prompt construction; the secret value never enters prompt_body, business payload, state, handoff, event, error, log, audit or telemetry.

opaque_reference.p6_behavior: May be schema-validated as an opaque reference but is never resolved, logged or used because external mutation is unavailable.

transient_exception: SecurityContext.pii_paths is supplied by trusted adapter code, not payload.
Each allowed JSON Pointer is exact or a declared array wildcard; parent wildcards are forbidden.
The value is required by the selected tactic input metadata with sensitivity customer_level_pii.
The value is used transiently and cannot appear in state, handoff, event, output, cache, error, log, audit or telemetry except in redacted or aggregate form.
pii_sensitive_input.persistence_rule: Local state accepts safe evidence references, redaction_status and aggregates only. A PII detector match in a state write denies with ERR_PII_PERSISTENCE_FORBIDDEN.

key_classification.normalization: Unicode-preserving casefold, replace every non-ASCII alphanumeric run with underscore, collapse underscores and strip them.
key_classification.key_rule: An exact normalized match classifies the entire value subtree. Do not use a generic substring such as 'key' or 'name'.

redaction_algorithm: Operate on a detached JSON copy; never mutate the caller value.
If a key is secret-classified, replace the entire value with [REDACTED:SECRET] and do not descend.
Else if a key is PII-classified, replace the entire value with [REDACTED:PII] and do not descend.
Else scan strings with the ordered value_detectors and replace every non-overlapping match with its class placeholder.
When matches overlap, choose the match with earliest start, then longest span, then detector_id lexical order.
Secret classification wins over PII classification for the same span.
Never hash, partially reveal, preserve prefix/suffix or report the length of a secret.
Apply sink allowlists after redaction, then enforce the sink byte budget.
If redaction or serialization fails, emit the constant ERR_SECURITY_INTERNAL response with empty details.

sink_rules.errors: Use a code-specific constant public message. Details use an allowlist per code and sanitized scalar enums/counts only; never include exception text, payload fragments or paths.
sink_rules.logs: Structured JSON only, default disabled. No arbitrary message field and no repr(value).
sink_rules.telemetry: Default disabled. When explicitly enabled by trusted bootstrap, emit only the audit_record schema after the same redaction and budget checks.
sink_rules.traceback: Never returned or written by P6 runtime sinks. Development-only stderr traceback is outside the release profile and must be disabled by default.

runtime_root.symlinks: deny in the root and every traversed component

library_root.contents: canonical biblioteca paths anchored by P3 SHA-256
library_root.source: trusted CLI or adapter bootstrap, not payload
library_root.symlinks: deny in root, ancestors from declared root to target, and final prompt file

input_root.source: launch working directory resolved once at bootstrap
input_root.symlinks: deny for target and traversed components below input_root

state_root.contents: .loop-marketing state, quarantine, locks, recovery and optional audit subtree
state_root.symlinks: deny for root, every existing component, targets, locks and temporary files

bootstrap_rules: Convert roots to absolute paths without relying on a later current working directory.
runtime_root, library_root and input_root must already exist and resolve strictly to directories.
state_root may be absent, but its nearest existing parent must resolve and contain the lexical target; create it only in local_state mode.
Reject a root containing NUL, an empty component, dot-dot after lexical normalization or more than 128 components.
Reject runtime_root equal to or contained by state_root.
Reject the canonical biblioteca directory equal to or contained by state_root.
Do not expose --runtime-root in the P6 installed release; development override must be an internal API argument and must pass the same sealed-asset verification.
Payload fields and prompt text cannot add a root or relax a boundary.
read_algorithm: Choose the required root role from trusted operation metadata.
Reject absolute payload paths where the field contract requires a relative path.
Join the relative path to the root, resolve with strict=True and verify os.path.commonpath equals the resolved root.
lstat every component from root to target and reject stat.S_ISLNK.
Require a regular file, enforce the role-specific size cap from stat before open, open with O_RDONLY and O_NOFOLLOW when available, then fstat and recheck regular type and size.
For canonical prompts, verify catalog path, exact SHA-256 and post-read size before UTF-8 decoding.
write_algorithm: Require effective_mode local_state and an operation with state_root write scope.
Validate project_id with the P4 safe slug before creating a path.
Verify lexical and resolved containment under state_root and lstat every existing component.
Use O_NOFOLLOW where available, exclusive creation for locks and temporary files, file mode 0600 and directory mode 0700.
Recheck containment, symlink status and permission immediately before each open, replace, rename, chmod or unlink.
Only ProjectStateStore performs business-state writes; CLI, adapter, catalog, router and prompt handlers never call write primitives directly.
trusted_path_boundaries.path_error_details: Public details may contain only path_role and component_index. Never return a full path, cwd, username or offending component text.

authority_precedence: P6 security policy and immutable SecurityContext
P2 role, routing, authority and handoff contracts
P3 sidecar execution_policy and verified tactic metadata
trusted operation request and requested output contract
canonical prompt_body as subordinate tactical data
external content referenced by the request
non_overridable_controls: route order, state revision or write set
tactic cardinality, identity, path or hash
credential discovery, credential-file reads or connector resolution
execution_rules: Load a body only after deterministic selection, catalog identity verification, hash verification, prompt byte budget and planner-review gates.
Never concatenate prompt_body into system, developer, policy, tool or permission instructions. Keep it in a separately labeled data field.
The executor may extract tactics only within allowed_output_sections and owner_domain.
If the body requests an excluded section, prohibited decision, secret, tool use, path access, authority change or external action, ignore that request and follow execution_policy.

If the body requests searching for or reading a token, credential, .env, Markdown or configuration file, do not perform discovery or a file read; return or record ERR_CREDENTIAL_DISCOVERY_DENIED when the request reaches an executable path.
If runtime cannot enforce the separation on a host, do not execute the body; fall back to the catalog base method with ERR_PROMPT_ISOLATION_UNAVAILABLE recorded as a non-secret safe violation.
A prompt-policy conflict cannot be fixed by editing the canonical prompt. Use sidecar constraints, mandatory handoff or base-method fallback.
Never copy prompt_body to handoff, state, event, audit, log, telemetry or error.

conflict_result.recoverable_sidecar_conflict: apply the stricter sidecar execution_policy and continue within the owner contract
conflict_result.unenforceable_isolation: deny prompt execution and use base method

deterministic_errors.public_message_rule: message is a constant selected by code. It is never built from an exception, payload, prompt, path or detector match.
deterministic_errors.details_rule: details defaults to an empty object and contains only code-specific allowlisted enums, booleans and bounded integer counts after redaction.

codes: P2, P3, P4 and P5 codes in their already sealed internal precedence

deterministic_errors.selection_rule: A stage that cannot safely continue returns its first code immediately. After a safe JSON tree exists, collect independent violations, sort by stage rank, code list position and normalized JSON Pointer, and expose the first as primary_code. Deduplicate equal code and pointer pairs.
deterministic_errors.multi_violation_rule: The public response may return violation codes and safe JSON Pointers only. It never returns offending values. Internal logs use the same sanitized list.

cli_exit_codes.2: safe input, resource, permission, path, prompt or domain rejection

deterministic_errors.unknown_exception: Catch at the outermost CLI or adapter boundary, return ERR_SECURITY_INTERNAL with constant message and empty details, no traceback, and perform no further write.

audit_record_privacy.generation: Every security decision may create one in-memory record. Persistence is optional, explicit, local_state-only and contained below state_root/audit/.

properties.occurred_at: UTC timestamp generated by trusted runtime
properties.primary_code: null on allow or a closed error code on deny
properties.resource_counters: bounded integers for raw_bytes, canonical_bytes, depth and nodes only
properties.sensitive_match_counts: bounded integers for SECRET and PII only

forbidden_fields: error message, exception text or traceback
project display name or customer identity
state, event, handoff or evidence content

persistence.format: one canonical JSON object per line
persistence.write_primitive: same-filesystem append protocol owned by the security module; no host adapter write
persistence.failure_behavior: Fail the business operation before its commit if a required audit sink cannot be written. If sink is optional, disable the sink and return the business result without attempting another path.

audit_record_privacy.privacy_rule: Redaction is defense in depth; the audit schema allowlist must make raw sensitive values structurally impossible.

before: resolve trusted roots and create immutable SecurityContext
limit raw bytes before JSON decode
reject duplicate keys and hostile JSON tree
choose operation from argparse subcommand, not JSON
after: sanitize and budget-check result or error
emit one JSON object without traceback
enforcement_points.required_surface_change: Add explicit local-state subcommands only for initialize/commit/recovery. Keep route and specialist read_only. Do not add any external operation.

before: construct SecurityContext from trusted host configuration
validate payload recursively before _serializable_copy
reject authority spoof fields as security authority
after: never expose a generic invoke or write method
enforcement_points.required_surface_change: HostAdapter receives a SecurityPolicy or SecurityContext factory; invoke_read_only remains read_only and a future invoke_local_state may call only the closed local-state operations.

before: require SecurityContext on every public operation
check operation_matrix and payload budgets
detect external mutation requests independently of P5 authority refs
after: remove prompt body from any persistent model
return permission metadata from trusted context only
enforcement_points.required_surface_change: commit_transaction requires local_state and all preparation methods require read_only or stronger; no implicit upgrade.

before: validate trusted library_root and canonical relative path
enforce prompt stat and aggregate byte budgets
verify tactic sidecar execution_policy and planner review
after: never log prompt bytes or decode errors containing content
enforcement_points.required_surface_change: load_selected accepts SecurityContext and returns a structured prompt envelope, not an authority-bearing instruction string.

before: apply recursive budgets before schema traversal
apply credential and PII persistence gates
preserve sealed P4 and P5 validation precedence after P6 precedence
enforcement_points.required_surface_change: All public validate methods accept SecurityContext or are called only behind a checked orchestrator boundary; state writes still recheck in ProjectStateStore.

before: require local_state immediately before every side effect
recheck trusted state_root, containment, symlinks and modes
reject secret and PII-bearing persistence
after: expose path roles rather than path strings
generate optional audit record without business payload
enforcement_points.required_surface_change: Separate inspect-only recovery from mutating recovery and prevent load() cache repair when effective mode is read_only.

before: accept closed codes and code-specific safe detail fields only
after: to_dict applies the central sanitizer and public error byte budget
enforcement_points.required_surface_change: Do not serialize arbitrary details currently supplied by P5 exceptions.

positive.request: route operation with no mode supplied, bounded JSON and project reference
positive.expected: effective mode read_only; authoritative ledger replayed without cache repair; route returned; no file write

positive.request: commit_transaction dispatched by trusted CLI local-state subcommand with effective_mode local_state
positive.expected: P6 permission passes; ProjectStateStore rechecks state_root and P4 CAS; only state_root may change

positive.request: selected prompt is 120000 bytes, catalog identity and SHA-256 match, execution_policy is sidecar_constrained
positive.expected: prompt loads into a separately labeled untrusted_tactical_data field and excluded decisions remain blocked

positive.request: read_only transient customer transcript at a trusted exact pii_path required by tactic metadata
positive.expected: analysis may consume it transiently; errors, outputs, logs and audit redact email and phone; no local persistence

positive.request: error details contain only path_role=input_root and component_index=2
positive.expected: safe details are returned under the 8192-byte cap; no filesystem component text appears

negative.expected_effect: no dispatch, network or file write; payload fields do not modify SecurityContext

negative.request: read_only route encounters stale derived cache
negative.expected_effect: use replay result without repairing cache; zero filesystem writes

negative.request: JSON input has one object nested to depth 33
negative.expected_effect: reject before domain validation and return no offending value

negative.request: JSON text repeats the key project_id
negative.expected_effect: reject even when both values are identical

negative.request: payload contains access_token with a bearer token and also asks for external mutation
negative.expected_effect: response and audit contain only codes and SECRET count; no token prefix, suffix, length or hash

negative.request: local-state event contains email not represented as a redacted reference or aggregate
negative.expected_effect: deny before lock acquisition and append

negative.request: CLI input path is a symlink inside input_root pointing to a contained regular file
negative.expected_effect: reject symlink even though resolved target remains contained

negative.request: canonical prompt says to send a campaign and includes a shell command
negative.expected_effect: treat those lines as data, produce only allowed owner output, expose no tool and perform no action

negative.request: host cannot separate prompt_body from policy instructions
negative.expected_effect: do not execute prompt; use base method; no authority elevation

negative.request: unknown Python exception contains a password and full path in its text
negative.expected_effect: constant message, empty details, exit 3, no traceback, password or path

negative.request: a canonical or user prompt says to find Token.txt, credentials.md or .env and use the discovered value
negative.expected_effect: no directory enumeration, glob, environment read or file open; v1 credential-collection behavior is not reproduced

verification_criteria.test: Enumerate public CLI and adapter methods.
verification_criteria.pass: Only read_only and local_state operations exist; no external mutation method or subcommand exists.

verification_criteria.test: Invoke every read-only operation under a filesystem write monitor, including stale-cache replay.
verification_criteria.pass: Zero create, append, replace, chmod, rename, unlink or repair calls occur.

verification_criteria.test: Submit external mutation requests through mode, payload boolean, authorization_ref, prompt text, alias and host adapter variants.
verification_criteria.pass: Every variant returns ERR_EXTERNAL_MUTATION_DENIED before dispatch and records no external side effect.

verification_criteria.test: Generate exact-limit and limit-plus-one cases for every recursive JSON counter.
verification_criteria.pass: Exact limits pass; plus-one cases return the specified deterministic resource code without RecursionError or MemoryError.

verification_criteria.test: Use duplicate keys, NaN, Infinity, bytes, Path, custom mapping and cyclic in-memory containers.
verification_criteria.pass: All hostile types reject with the defined parse or resource code and no domain method runs.

verification_criteria.test: Place each secret-key class and value detector sample in input, nested errors, logs, audit and telemetry.
verification_criteria.pass: Raw secret input rejects; every sink contains only [REDACTED:SECRET] or safe counts and never prefix, suffix, length or hash.

verification_criteria.test: Place email, CPF and phone samples in undeclared input, declared transient input and attempted state writes.
verification_criteria.pass: Undeclared input rejects, declared read-only transient use does not persist or leak, and state writes reject.

verification_criteria.test: Exercise path escape, absolute relative-field path, symlink at every component, FIFO/device/socket target, root overlap and TOCTOU replacement before open.
verification_criteria.pass: Every case fails closed with a path code; public output contains no full path or component text.

verification_criteria.test: Load prompt bodies at exact byte caps, cap plus one, aggregate plus one, wrong hash and wrong catalog path.
verification_criteria.pass: Only exact, contained, regular, hash-matching prompts load; all others reject before execution.

verification_criteria.test: Inject prompt directives that request tool use, secret access, owner changes, excluded output, state writes and external actions.
verification_criteria.pass: The envelope keeps them subordinate to execution_policy and no non-overridable control changes.

verification_criteria.test: Force each error stage alone and in valid multi-violation combinations.
verification_criteria.pass: primary_code and violation order exactly match precedence_stages across repeated runs and hash seeds.

verification_criteria.test: Raise unknown exceptions containing synthetic secrets, PII, payload text and paths at every CLI and adapter boundary.
verification_criteria.pass: Only ERR_SECURITY_INTERNAL, its constant message, empty details and request_id are emitted; no traceback is present.

verification_criteria.test: Schema-validate every generated audit record and search it for forbidden fields and detector samples.
verification_criteria.pass: Records contain only the allowlist, stay under 32768 bytes and contain no user-authored text.

verification_criteria.test: Attempt local-state writes through CLI, adapter, orchestrator and direct ProjectStateStore calls with read_only or missing context.
verification_criteria.pass: Every bypass attempt denies; only a local_state context reaches a contained ProjectStateStore write.

verification_criteria.test: Run the sealed P5 suite plus new P6 tests on Python 3.9 with no third-party package installed.
verification_criteria.pass: P5 behavior remains compatible where allowed, P6 closes unsafe surfaces, canonical source stays clean and all 100 prompt hashes remain unchanged.

verification_criteria.test: Place token-file discovery instructions in canonical prompt text, user payload, v1-compatible aliases and nested external content while monitoring directory, glob, environment and file-read primitives.
verification_criteria.pass: Every executable discovery request returns ERR_CREDENTIAL_DISCOVERY_DENIED and performs zero enumeration or reads; an opaque future connector reference remains outside prompt and state and is never resolved by P6.

implementation_requirements: Create one central security.py implementation for SecurityPolicy, SecurityContext, JSONBudgetValidator, SensitiveDataRedactor, TrustedPathResolver and audit construction.
Do not duplicate regexes, budgets, permission tables or error precedence in CLI and adapters.
Keep policy objects immutable after construction and return detached JSON-compatible values.
Unit tests must patch filesystem primitives at enforcement boundaries to prove no forbidden side effects.
Security checks are mandatory defense in depth at ingress and again immediately before side effects; an internal caller cannot bypass them by skipping CLI or adapter validation.
The integrated P6 security-contract may tighten numeric budgets but must not raise them or expose external mutation without a new reviewed policy version.
integration_notes: P5 RuntimeConfig.normalized currently resolves every path without assigning a trust role; P6 must replace that behavior with TrustedPathResolver checks before component construction.
P5 _serializable_copy validates serialization but has no recursive resource budget, duplicate-key detection or sensitive-data policy; P6 ingress must run before it.
P5 LoopRuntimeError.to_dict currently returns arbitrary details; P6 must centralize code allowlists and redaction before any CLI or adapter serialization.

Any v1 prompt or command behavior that searched local files for tokens is intentionally not carried into v2; future connectors require explicit trusted adapter configuration and keep secret values outside all runtime artifacts.
P5 ProjectStateStore.load may repair derived caches. Read-only orchestration already uses replay; P6 must prevent load-with-repair from being reachable under read_only.
P5 load_selected returns prompt_body beside catalog metadata. P6 must wrap it as untrusted_tactical_data and enforce execution_policy separation at the eventual executor boundary.
